required-output.evidence: A complete Japanese X Article, three operational sections, and repository CTA exist.

rights-and-safety.evidence: The source is attributed through the fixture; no protected body expression is reproduced and all performance language is qualified.

anti-clone.evidence: The output changes the topic, claims, mechanism details, section language, examples, and CTA.

intent-alignment.evidence: The article clearly changes the completion model from writing a skill file to running an evaluated production system.

functional-structure.evidence: Each section performs a distinct function: forward test, rubric freeze, and root-layer repair.

claim-and-evidence.evidence: The 80/60/three-round contract traces to the repository implementation and is explicitly framed as an internal standard, not an outcome guarantee.

x-readability-and-impression-proxy.evidence: The opening is specific, paragraphs are mobile-scannable, headings carry information, and the CTA is low-friction. Live performance remains untested.

originality-and-anti-clone.evidence: Only the functional relation is transferred; no distinctive source phrase, numerical spectacle, template names, or examples remain.

unresolved_assumptions: The pre-publication score is a quality proxy and cannot establish impression performance.
One source example is insufficient to promote the generated skill beyond experimental status.
